code: @Manual{,   title = {rmoriebricklayer: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback},   author = {Vansh Singh Ruhela},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/rootcoder007/rmorie-bricklayer}, }

what: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
title: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
text: Brick-proof, reproducible data bundles R. rmoriebricklayer resolves open-data sources, records verifies provenance, validates downloaded data pinned schema, falls back schema-driven synthetic data real source unreachable — analysis result can traced back exact inputs.

title: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
text: CKAN resolution — resolve_via_ckan() / resolve_via_ckan_search() locate resources portal’s package_show / package_search endpoints. Provenance — load_provenance(), make_manifest(), record(), write_manifest_json(), write_summary_txt() capture every run manifest plus plain-language summary. Integrity — sha256_file() / verify_sha256() hash verify downloads; download_data() / friendly_download() fetch Wayback Machine fallback. Schema validation — validate_schema() / apply_schema_validation() check data pinned schema. Synthetic fallback — make_synthetic_column() / make_synthetic_csv() generate schema-driven stand-ins real source , pipeline still runs end--end.

title: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
code: library(rmoriebricklayer)  prov <- load_provenance("provenance.json")     # pinned source + schema + hash res  <- resolve_via_ckan(prov)                  # find the resource on the portal path <- friendly_download(res$url, "data.csv")  # download (Wayback fallback) verify_sha256(path, prov$sha256)                # integrity check df   <- validate_schema(read.csv(path), prov)   # schema-validated data frame  man  <- make_manifest(project = "my-study") record(man, "input", path)                      # trace the input write_manifest_json(man, "manifest.json")

title: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
text: rmoriebricklayer reproducibility / provenance layer MORIE ecosystem, alongside rmorie, rmoriedata, rmorielite.

title: Reproducible Data Bundles with Provenance and Fallback
text: AGPL-3.0--later.

what: Apply Schema Validation, Stopping on Fatal Issues — apply_schema_validation
title: Apply Schema Validation, Stopping on Fatal Issues — apply_schema_validation
text: Runs validate_schema() acts result: fatal issues raise error via stop(), warning-severity issues emit warning().

title: Apply Schema Validation, Stopping on Fatal Issues — apply_schema_validation
Apply Schema Validation, Stopping on Fatal Issues — apply_schema_validation
text: df_raw data frame validate. provenance provenance list returned load_provenance().

title: Apply Schema Validation, Stopping on Fatal Issues — apply_schema_validation
text: Invisibly, TRUE issues found FALSE otherwise. Errors fatal issue present.

title: Download a File — download_data
text: Thin wrapper around utils::download.file() returns target path invisibly composes pipelines.

title: Download a File — download_data
Download a File — download_data
text: url URL download. target_path Destination path disk. mode Write mode passed utils::download.file(); defaults "wb" (binary) cross-platform safety. quiet Logical; suppress progress output. Defaults FALSE.

title: Download a File — download_data
text: target_path, returned invisibly.

what: Download a File With Diagnostic Error Messages — friendly_download
title: Download a File With Diagnostic Error Messages — friendly_download
text: Wraps utils::download.file() , failure, prints plain-language guidance common academic corporate network problems (rate limiting, TLS-inspection VPNs, DNS failures, timeouts, HTTP 403). Optionally retries Wayback Machine snapshot URL.

title: Download a File With Diagnostic Error Messages — friendly_download
Download a File With Diagnostic Error Messages — friendly_download
text: url URL download. target_path Destination path disk. attempt_wayback Wayback Machine snapshot URL tried fallback primary download fails. NULL (default) snapshot resolved automatically via wayback_snapshot_url(); pass explicit URL override lookup, "" disable fallback entirely.

title: Download a File With Diagnostic Error Messages — friendly_download
text: TRUE either primary download Wayback fallback succeeds, otherwise FALSE.

what: Load a Pinned Data-Provenance Record — load_provenance
title: Load a Pinned Data-Provenance Record — load_provenance
text: Reads data_provenance.json file describing project's pinned data source: CKAN endpoint, resource name pattern, expected SHA256, Wayback snapshot, schema, synthetic-data recipe.

title: Load a Pinned Data-Provenance Record — load_provenance
Load a Pinned Data-Provenance Record — load_provenance
text: path Path provenance JSON file.

title: Load a Pinned Data-Provenance Record — load_provenance
text: parsed provenance nested list (via jsonlite::fromJSON() simplifyVector = FALSE), NULL file exist.

title: Construct a Reproducibility Manifest — make_manifest
text: Creates empty manifest object accumulates cross-check entries via record() later serialized write_manifest_json().

title: Construct a Reproducibility Manifest — make_manifest
Construct a Reproducibility Manifest — make_manifest
text: meta named list run metadata (e.g. project, author, run_at, os, r_version, synthetic).

title: Construct a Reproducibility Manifest — make_manifest
text: manifest list elements meta empty results list.

what: Generate One Synthetic Column From a Spec — make_synthetic_column
title: Generate One Synthetic Column From a Spec — make_synthetic_column
text: Builds single synthetic data column according column spec drawn provenance synthetic recipe. Supported types "sample" (categorical, optionally weighted), "bernoulli" (two-label draw optional per-row base rate), "poisson" (counts floor), "id_pattern" (templated IDs, optionally per-year sequenced), "sequence" (running integer sequence).

title: Generate One Synthetic Column From a Spec — make_synthetic_column
code: make_synthetic_column(spec, n, ctx = list(), base_p = NULL)

title: Generate One Synthetic Column From a Spec — make_synthetic_column
text: spec list describing column; recognised fields depend spec$type (e.g. values, weights, p, p_with_baserate, labels, lambda, min, pattern, year_col, ). n Number values generate. ctx Named list already-generated columns, letting later columns (id_pattern year_col) reference earlier ones. Defaults empty list. base_p Optional numeric vector per-row latent propensities used "bernoulli" type add row-level variation.

title: Generate One Synthetic Column From a Spec — make_synthetic_column
text: vector length n requested column type. Errors unknown type.

what: Generate a Synthetic CSV From a Schema Recipe — make_synthetic_csv
title: Generate a Synthetic CSV From a Schema Recipe — make_synthetic_csv
text: Generates reproducible synthetic data set schema$synthetic_recipe block provenance object writes CSV. Columns produced declaration order later columns can reference earlier ones, shared per-row latent propensity drives Bernoulli columns, optional row-replication block expands per-person rows.

title: Generate a Synthetic CSV From a Schema Recipe — make_synthetic_csv
code: make_synthetic_csv(schema, out_path, n_rows = NULL, seed = NULL)

title: Generate a Synthetic CSV From a Schema Recipe — make_synthetic_csv
text: schema synthetic recipe (list columns, optional n_rows, seed, row_replication). out_path Path CSV written. n_rows Number rows (persons, replicating) generate. Defaults schema$n_rows, 50000. seed Random seed reproducibility. Defaults schema$seed, 91735246.

title: Generate a Synthetic CSV From a Schema Recipe — make_synthetic_csv
text: Invisibly, list path, rows (rows written), seed used.

what: Record a Cross-Check Result in a Manifest — record
title: Record a Cross-Check Result in a Manifest — record
text: Appends one named cross-check entry manifest, classifying PASS, DIFFER, INFO, printing formatted line console, returning updated manifest.

title: Record a Cross-Check Result in a Manifest — record
code: record(   manifest,   name,   observed,   expected,   tol = 1e-04,   group = "general",   synthetic = FALSE )

title: Record a Cross-Check Result in a Manifest — record
text: manifest manifest returned make_manifest(). name Unique name cross-check; used result key. observed observed value (numeric otherwise). expected expected value compare . tol Numeric tolerance; numeric pair within tol PASS. Defaults 0.0001. group Optional grouping label entry. Defaults "general". synthetic Logical; TRUE entry marked INFO comparison synthetic data meaningful.

title: Record a Cross-Check Result in a Manifest — record
text: updated manifest, returned calls can chained.

what: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_show — resolve_via_ckan
title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_show — resolve_via_ckan
text: Queries CKAN package_show endpoint recorded provenance object returns URL first resource whose name matches provenance's name-match pattern. CKAN powers data.ontario.ca, data.gov.uk, data.gov, government open-data portals, recovers current download URL even underlying resource UUID replaced.

title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_show — resolve_via_ckan
Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_show — resolve_via_ckan
text: provenance provenance list returned load_provenance(). Must contain dataset$ckan_api_endpoint resource$name_match_pattern.

title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_show — resolve_via_ckan
text: matched resource URL character string, NULL endpoint missing, request fails, CKAN reports failure, resource name matches.

what: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_search — resolve_via_ckan_search
title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_search — resolve_via_ckan_search
text: Fallback resolve_via_ckan() dataset slug changed. Derives CKAN portal base URL provenance's package_show endpoint, runs package_search query (resource$search_query, derived name-match pattern), returns URL first matching resource, preferring CSV format specified.

title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_search — resolve_via_ckan_search
Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_search — resolve_via_ckan_search
text: provenance provenance list returned load_provenance(). Uses resource$search_query, resource$name_match_pattern, resource$format, dataset$ckan_api_endpoint.

title: Resolve a Download URL via CKAN package_search — resolve_via_ckan_search
text: matched resource URL character string, NULL query base URL can derived, request fails, nothing matches.

what: Compute a File's SHA256 Digest — sha256_file
title: Compute a File's SHA256 Digest — sha256_file
text: Returns SHA256 digest file lowercase hex string, using digest package. Used record verify data provenance.

title: Compute a File's SHA256 Digest — sha256_file
Compute a File's SHA256 Digest — sha256_file
text: path Path file hash.

title: Compute a File's SHA256 Digest — sha256_file
text: SHA256 digest character string.

what: Validate a Data Frame Against a Provenance Schema — validate_schema
title: Validate a Data Frame Against a Provenance Schema — validate_schema
text: Checks raw data frame schema block provenance object: required columns, row-count bounds, allowed categorical value sets. Returns issues found rather raising, caller decides react.

title: Validate a Data Frame Against a Provenance Schema — validate_schema
Validate a Data Frame Against a Provenance Schema — validate_schema
text: df_raw data frame validate. provenance provenance list returned load_provenance(). schema block may contain expected_columns, structural_invariants (min_data_rows, max_data_rows), expected_value_sets (named list allowed values per column).

title: Validate a Data Frame Against a Provenance Schema — validate_schema
text: named list issues; issue list severity ("fatal" "warning") human-readable message. zero-length list means data frame clean.

what: Verify a File's SHA256 Against an Expected Digest — verify_sha256
title: Verify a File's SHA256 Against an Expected Digest — verify_sha256
text: Computes SHA256 digest file (via digest package) compares expected value pinned provenance.

title: Verify a File's SHA256 Against an Expected Digest — verify_sha256
Verify a File's SHA256 Against an Expected Digest — verify_sha256
text: path Path file hash. expected_sha expected SHA256 digest, lowercase hex string.

title: Verify a File's SHA256 Against an Expected Digest — verify_sha256
text: list actual (computed digest), expected (value passed ), match (logical; TRUE identical).

what: Resolve a Wayback Machine snapshot URL — wayback_snapshot_url
title: Resolve a Wayback Machine snapshot URL — wayback_snapshot_url
text: Queries Internet Archive availability API (http://archive.org/wayback/available) closest archived snapshot url returns directly-downloadable snapshot URL, NULL snapshot exists lookup fails. shared fetch failsafe wider morie package family relies : callers attempt live source first fall back snapshot source unreachable.

title: Resolve a Wayback Machine snapshot URL — wayback_snapshot_url
Resolve a Wayback Machine snapshot URL — wayback_snapshot_url
text: url original source URL look . timestamp Optional 14-digit YYYYMMDDhhmmss target; API returns snapshot closest . Defaults recent.

title: Resolve a Wayback Machine snapshot URL — wayback_snapshot_url
text: character scalar snapshot URL, NULL.

what: Write a Manifest to JSON — write_manifest_json
title: Write a Manifest to JSON — write_manifest_json
text: Serializes manifest pretty-printed JSON file via jsonlite package.

title: Write a Manifest to JSON — write_manifest_json
Write a Manifest to JSON — write_manifest_json
text: manifest manifest returned make_manifest() / built record(). path Destination path JSON file.

title: Write a Manifest to JSON — write_manifest_json
text: path, returned invisibly.

what: Write a Plain-Language Run Summary — write_summary_txt
title: Write a Plain-Language Run Summary — write_summary_txt
text: Writes human-readable SUMMARY.txt output directory, covering run metadata, exact absolute paths used, result counts, files produced, optional notes, contact, licence lines.

title: Write a Plain-Language Run Summary — write_summary_txt
code: write_summary_txt(   manifest,   output_dir,   paths,   what_was_done = NULL,   contact = NULL,   licence = NULL )

title: Write a Plain-Language Run Summary — write_summary_txt
text: manifest manifest returned make_manifest(); meta supplies project/author/run details. output_dir Directory write SUMMARY.txt list produced files . paths named list absolute paths report (e.g. bundle, input, results, analysis_script, provenance). what_was_done Optional character vector bullet points describing run . contact Optional contact string appended summary. licence Optional licence string appended summary.

title: Write a Plain-Language Run Summary — write_summary_txt
text: path written SUMMARY.txt, returned invisibly.
